text: In this curiously repetitive passage, from here to the end of the chapter, the Latin version follows the Hebrew text, which is here so different from the Septuagint Greek as to suggest the possibility of a very early corruption in the manuscripts.
This verse does not appear in the Septuagint Greek at all.

It is difficult to attach any sense to this verse unless it is treated as a question, not a statement. ‘Their corpses lawless yet’; literally, ‘their lawlessnesses were upon their bones’; the absence of circumcision is perhaps referred to.
The mention of the Edomites (who practised circumcision) is unexpected. According to the Septuagint Greek, we return in this verse and the next to the Assyrians.
